channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: SBA-7a Loan Stip Fraud Detection: Post-PPP Lessons
item.description: Originally published at htpbe.tech. The version on htpbe.tech stays in sync with the latest detection algorithm — refer to it for the canonical text. The PPP wave was, in retrospect, the largest...

item.title: Server Components vs Client Components: The Mental Model Shift Every Vite Developer Needs
item.description: Introduction If you have been building applications using Vite and standard React for the last few years, you’ve likely mastered the Single Page Application (SPA) paradigm. You know that...

item.title: San Francisco Demands Apple and Google Delete AI ‘Nudify’ Apps From App Stores
item.description: The City Attorney’s Office sent the tech giants cease-and-desist letters this week telling them to stop profiting from 13 “face-swap” apps that are overwhelmingly used to target women and girls.

item.title: MAD-SHOW Lighting Control Software: A Comprehensive Guide from Beginner to Advanced Features
item.description: This is a complete introductory guide to the MAD-SHOW lighting control software. Whether you are a beginner new to lighting programming or a professional lighting designer seeking a lighting...

item.title: My Personal AI Stack in 2026
item.description: Ask ten AI developers what tools they use, and you'll probably get ten different answers. The AI ecosystem is evolving so quickly that it's easy to believe you need every new framework, model, and...

item.title: The AI Blind Spot: Why "It Works" Isn't the Same as "It's Safe to Launch"
item.description: A few months ago, a founder posted about the SaaS he'd just shipped — built entirely with an AI coding assistant, not a line of it typed by hand. He was proud of it, and he had every right to be....

item.title: How to Choose the Right AI Model for Your Application
item.description: Integrating multiple AI models is not difficult. The real challenge is determining which model is best suited for each task. Many developers choose models based only on benchmarks, brand...

item.title: Introduction to Probo-ui — Write HTML Entirely in Python series
item.description: A tutorial series, DEV.to blog series — from your first HTML element to production-grade User Interfaces, all in pure Python. Modern Python web frameworks force developers into a split workflow:...

item.title: Why Static Accessibility Scanners Miss What AI Agents Hit
item.description: This button passes every automated accessibility scan we've thrown at it: <button class="btn-primary" type="button"> Check availability </button> And it breaks every AI agent that tries to book a...

item.title: Add newsletter subscriptions to Rails 8 signups
item.description: Users are creating accounts on your new Saas. Yay (and not just family and friends or bots). Yay! Now comes the next step from every marketing handbook: capturing newsletter subscriptions. This...

item.title: Two Bugs, Two Strangers, One Week: What Shipping Early Actually Buys You
item.description: A week ago I put a rough, honestly-a-bit-thin version of PulseWatch in front of real people for the first time. Within days, two different strangers — independently, unprompted — found two real...

item.title: There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it.
item.description: Perimenopause has entered the chat. Perimenopause—and its better-known relative, menopause—used to be considered taboo. Not anymore, thanks at least in part to TV doctors and social media...

item.title: A Humanoid Company Backed by Eric Trump Is Preparing Its Robots for War
item.description: The CEO of Foundation Future Industries, which counts the president’s son as its chief strategy adviser, tells WIRED it’s exploring some “kinetic things.”

item.title: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
item.description: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people...

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly patched security flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV)...

item.title: Why Apple Sued OpenAI, New York Takes on Data Centers, and What to Know about Cyclosporiasis
item.description: On today’s Uncanny Valley, we unpack OpenAI’s ongoing drama, both legal and reputational, and whether these developments could further hurt the company—particularly in its fight against Anthropic.

item.title: HP fined 1.4 billion rupees for “cartelization” of ink cartridges, toner, PCs
item.description: Resellers threatened to ditch HP printing supplies for counterfeits.

item.title: Fortnite is getting a bunch of AI-powered ‘personas’
item.description: Get ready for more AI characters in Fortnite. Developer Epic Games is going to let Fortnite creators publish experiences featuring characters with AI-powered voices starting on July 30th, and...

item.title: Samsung’s 55-inch Frame art TV is $200 cheaper than usual
item.description: Samsung’s Frame is different from your average 4K TV. Its biggest selling point involves what it does when you aren’t actively using it. It can display art, turning your living room into a...

item.title: Netflix says around 300 titles used generative AI
item.description: Netflix says roughly 300 titles on its platform used generative AI, most of which occurred in post-production. The streaming service revealed the news in its second-quarter earnings report...

item.title: Why are people buying so many CDs?
item.description: CD sales are apparently going up, reportedly thanks to fans realizing they're an affordable way to support their favorite artists. According to a new report from research firm Luminate, 16.3...

item.title: Now, even Russia's most elite hackers are using Clickfix to infect devices
item.description: The social-engineering technique has primarily been a tool of financially motivated criminals.

item.title: Kalshi says it caught Trump’s teleprompter operator insider trading
item.description: Kalshi users betting on what President Donald Trump would say during his speeches were reportedly up against tough competition: the president's teleprompter operator. ABC News reports that federal...

item.title: Here’s Why Anthropic Is Pushing States to Regulate AI Faster
item.description: The company endorsed landmark AI transparency laws in California and New York last year, but its head of US state and local policy says they may already be outdated.

item.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
item.description: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from...

item.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
item.description: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

item.title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
item.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul might have just signed a moratorium on new AI data centers in the state, but she's not against using the technology herself. During an interview with Bloomberg's Odd...

item.title: Ecovacs’ self-cleaning Deebot X11 has hit a new low price
item.description: Sometimes it feels like keeping your floors clean is one of those never-ending chores, which is why it's nice to have a versatile robot vacuum take it off your hands. The Ecovacs Deebot X11...

item.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
item.description: Owen Flowers, 18, and Thalha Jubair, 20, were each sentenced to five and a half years at Woolwich Crown Court on Thursday, 16 July 2026, for the 2024 hack of Transport for London. The attack left...

item.title: Google is better than Apple at playing the AI regulations game
item.description: Today, the European Union ordered Google to give its AI rivals greater access to Android, the open-source operating system that powers billions of devices worldwide. The demand is hardly...
